channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Verify as You Go: An LLM-Powered Browser Extension for Fake News Detection
item.description: arXiv:2603.05519v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rampant spread of fake news in the digital age poses serious risks to public trust and democratic institutions, underscoring the need for effective, transparent, and user-centered detection tools. Existing browser extensions often fall short due t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, retrieval, paper, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Attention Meets Reachability: Structural Equivalence and Efficiency in Grammar-Constrained LLM Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2603.05540v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study grammar-constrained decoding (GCD) as a coupling between an autoregressive next-token distribution and a reachability oracle over a pushdown system compiled from a context-free grammar (CFG). We prove an oracle invariance theorem: language-eq...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, study, model, attention, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NOTAI.AI: Explainable Detection of Machine-Generated Text via Curvature and Feature Attribution
item.description: arXiv:2603.05617v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present NOTAI.AI, an explainable framework for machine-generated text detection that extends Fast-DetectGPT by integrating curvature-based signals with neural and stylometric features in a supervised setting. The system combines 17 interpretable fe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, GPT, framework, arxiv, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FreeTxt-Vi: A Benchmarked Vietnamese-English Toolkit for Segmentation, Sentiment, and Summarisation
item.description: arXiv:2603.05690v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: FreeTxt-Vi is a free and open source web based toolkit for creating and analysing bilingual Vietnamese English text collections. Positioned at the intersection of corpus linguistics and natural language processing NLP it enables users to build explore...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, model, analysis, arxiv, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Let's Talk, Not Type: An Oral-First Multi-Agent Architecture for Guaran\'i
item.description: arXiv:2603.05743v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although artificial intelligence (AI) and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) systems are often presented as universal solutions, their design remains predominantly text-first, underserving primarily oral languages and indigenous communities. This positi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, study, RAG, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RoboLayout: Differentiable 3D Scene Generation for Embodied Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.05522v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in vision language models (VLMs) have shown strong potential for spatial reasoning and 3D scene layout generation from open-ended language instructions. However, generating layouts that are not only semantically coherent but also feasi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, instruction, paper, vision, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The World Won't Stay Still: Programmable Evolution for Agent Benchmarks
item.description: arXiv:2603.05910v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-powered agents fulfill user requests by interacting with environments, querying data, and invoking tools in a multi-turn process. Yet, most existing benchmarks assume static environments with fixed schemas and toolsets, neglecting the evolutionary...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, study, framework, arxiv, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DeepFact: Co-Evolving Benchmarks and Agents for Deep Research Factuality
item.description: arXiv:2603.05912v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Search-augmented LLM agents can produce deep research reports (DRRs), but verifying claim-level factuality remains challenging. Existing fact-checkers are primarily designed for general-domain, factoid-style atomic claims, and there is no benchmark to...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, study, vision, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Interactive Multi-Agent System for Evaluation of New Product Concepts
item.description: arXiv:2603.05980v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Product concept evaluation is a critical stage that determines strategic resource allocation and project success in enterprises. However, traditional expert-led approaches face limitations such as subjective bias and high time and cost requirements. T...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, retrieval, product, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VDCook:DIY video data cook your MLLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.05539v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce VDCook: a self-evolving video data operating system, a configurable video data construction platform for researchers and vertical domain teams. Users initiate data requests via natural language queries and adjustable parameters (scale, re...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> platform, retrieval, model, arxiv, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: IntSeqBERT: Learning Arithmetic Structure in OEIS via Modulo-Spectrum Embeddings
item.description: arXiv:2603.05556v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Integer sequences in the OEIS span values from single-digit constants to astronomical factorials and exponentials, making prediction challenging for standard tokenised models that cannot handle out-of-vocabulary values or exploit periodic arithmetic s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, analysis, arxiv, embedding, transformer | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Aggregative Semantics for Quantitative Bipolar Argumentation Frameworks
item.description: arXiv:2603.06067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Formal argumentation is being used increasingly in artificial intelligence as an effective and understandable way to model potentially conflicting pieces of information, called arguments, and identify so-called acceptable arguments depending on a chos...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, model, framework, arxiv, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Offline Materials Optimization with CliqueFlowmer
item.description: arXiv:2603.06082v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in deep learning inspired neural network-based approaches to computational materials discovery (CMD). A plethora of problems in this field involve finding materials that optimize a target property. Nevertheless, the increasingly popula...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, research, transformer, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FuseDiff: Symmetry-Preserving Joint Diffusion for Dual-Target Structure-Based Drug Design
item.description: arXiv:2603.05567v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Dual-target structure-based drug design aims to generate a single ligand together with two pocket-specific binding poses, each compatible with a corresponding target pocket, enabling polypharmacological therapies with improved efficacy and reduced res...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, arxiv, API, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Why Depth Matters in Parallelizable Sequence Models: A Lie Algebraic View
item.description: arXiv:2603.05573v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scalable sequence models, such as Transformer variants and structured state-space models, often trade expressivity power for sequence-level parallelism, which enables efficient training. Here we examine the bounds on error and how error scales when mo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Real-Time AI Service Economy: A Framework for Agentic Computing Across the Continuum
item.description: arXiv:2603.05614v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-time AI services increasingly operate across the device-edge-cloud continuum, where autonomous AI agents generate latency-sensitive workloads, orchestrate multi-stage processing pipelines, and compete for shared resources under policy and governa...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, study, model, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Autocorrelation effects in a stochastic-process model for decision making via time series
item.description: arXiv:2603.05559v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decision makers exploiting photonic chaotic dynamics obtained by semiconductor lasers provide an ultrafast approach to solving multi-armed bandit problems by using a temporal optical signal as the driving source for sequential decisions. In such syste...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Efficient and Stable Ocean State Forecasting: A Continuous-Time Koopman Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.05560v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate the Continuous-Time Koopman Autoencoder (CT-KAE) as a lightweight surrogate model for long-horizon ocean state forecasting in a two-layer quasi-geostrophic (QG) system. By projecting nonlinear dynamics into a latent space governed by a ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, transformer, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Cultural Perspectives and Expectations for Generative AI: A Global Survey Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.05723v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: There is a lack of empirical evidence about global attitudes around whether and how GenAI should represent cultures. This paper assesses understandings and beliefs about culture as it relates to GenAI from a large-scale global survey. We gathered data...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: JAWS: Enhancing Long-term Rollout of Neural Operators via Spatially-Adaptive Jacobian Regularization
item.description: arXiv:2603.05538v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data-driven surrogate models improve the efficiency of simulating continuous dynamical systems, yet their autoregressive rollouts are often limited by instability and spectral blow-up. While global regularization techniques can enforce contractive dyn...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, memory, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
